question_text: A teacher sets up an experiment comparing two electromagnets. Electromagnet X is wrapped tightly around a solid iron core, while Electromagnet Y is wrapped around a hollow plastic tube. Both have identical coils with the same number of turns and carry the same current. Which statement best explains the difference in their magnetic strengths?

options.label: A
options.text: Electromagnet X is stronger because iron is ferromagnetic and becomes magnetised, greatly amplifying the magnetic field produced by the coil.

options.label: B
options.text: Electromagnet Y is stronger because the hollow tube allows the magnetic field to spread out more effectively.

options.label: C
options.text: Both electromagnets produce exactly the same strength because they have identical coils and current.

options.label: D
options.text: Electromagnet X is stronger only because it is made of solid material rather than hollow material.

explanation: The iron core in Electromagnet X is ferromagnetic, meaning it becomes magnetised when placed in a magnetic field and significantly amplifies the electromagnet's strength. The plastic tube in Electromagnet Y cannot be magnetised, so only the coil's own magnetic field is produced. This demonstrates why selecting the correct core material is crucial for electromagnet design.
